title: Agro Transformation for Mimulus in Planta Transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"it24","text":"This protocol is part of a collection for Mimulus in planta transformation. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":42,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":27,"length":47}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"0992A0B087DC11E9936523EAC2E12BB2","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/mi-3tkgnkw/protocols"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"b9utv","text":"For Safety Warnings and Hazard Information please refer to the SDS (Satety Data Sheet). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Please select from the following two options: 1. Agro Transformation - Using Electroporator 2. Agro Transformation - Using Freeze -Thaw Method
Chill 2-mm electroporation cuvette on ice.
Aliquot 1 mL, LB into eppie tube.
Add 1.5 µL, plasmid DNA to agro cells.
Transfer the agro + plasmid DNA to the cuvette.
Electroporate: (Bio-Rad Electroporator program Agr) Capacitance:25 µF Voltage:2.4 kV Resistance: 200 Ohm Pulse length: 5 msec
Immediately add 1 mL, LB to the cuvette.
Transferto an eppie tube and shake at 28 °C - 30 °C for 1-2 hrs 01:00:00 .
Plate 100 µL on LB + Kan + Gent + Rif plate and incubate for 48:00:00 at 28 °C - 30 °C .
Thaw agro competentcells on ice (stored at -80 °C )– Agrobacterium GV3101
Thaw competent Agrobacterium on ice (use 250 µL per transformation reaction) and add 10 µL, standard E. coli miniprep plasmid .
Incubate the mixture on ice for 00:05:00 .
Transfer mixture to liquid nitrogen and incubate for 00:05:00 .
Incubate the mixture for a further 00:08:00 in a 37 °C dry bath.
Add 1 mL, LB to each tube.
Shake at 28 °C for at least 02:00:00 .
Collect the cells as follows.
Spin at for 00:01:00 in a microcentrifuge.
Take 750 µL, LB out.
Resuspend the pellet with the remaining LB.
Spread the cells on LB agar plates containing the appropriate antibiotic.
Incubate cells for 48:00:00 at 28 °C .
creator: Andrea Sweigart
